title: Scenario
description: A single decision-grounding benchmark scenario: a project corpus, an agent task, the binding decision(s), typed relationships (including supersedes), and a gold label written blind to which arm produced which output.

scenario_id.description: Stable slug; matches the scenario directory name.

version.description: Scenario revision, frozen once results exist (pre-registration).

scenario_type.description: Taxonomy class. simple_adherence cases are expected ties; the discriminating types (superseded_decision, prohibition_at_point_of_action, conflicting_scoped) are over-weighted; negative_control catches hallucinated prohibitions.

expected_tie.description: True when all arms are expected to score equally (e.g. easy single-decision cases). Labeled explicitly so ties are not mistaken for signal.

prompt.description: What the agent is asked to do, in the user's voice.

proposed_action.description: The concrete action the agent is on the verge of taking; the point at which a prior decision may bind.

binding_decisions.description: IDs of the decision artifacts that actually govern this task. Empty for a negative control (no decision binds).

relationships.description: Typed edges between artifacts. supersedes drives the superseded-decision class; conflicts_with / scopes drive the conflicting-scoped class.

verdict.description: Whether the proposed_action is permitted or prohibited given the corpus.

governing_decision.description: The decision that determines the verdict. null encodes the negative control: no decision governs, so inventing a prohibition is an error.

prohibited_actions.description: Action kinds/targets the agent must NOT propose.

required_actions.description: Action kinds/targets the agent SHOULD propose to be adherent.

rationale.description: Why the gold label is what it is. Written blind to arm outputs.

path.description: Path to the markdown artifact, relative to the scenario directory.

supersedes.description: IDs of artifacts this one supersedes. Mirrors the typed supersedes edge; redundant with relationships[] for retrieval convenience.

filler.description: True for illustrative padding artifacts used only to raise corpus size N. Never affects the gold label.
